title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Mercimek Corbasi: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Mercimek Corbasi, a comforting Turkish Red Lentil Soup. Our easy recipe brings the flavors of Turkey right to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup
seo.h1: Authentic Mercimek Corbasi: Turkish Red Lentil Soup
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming Mercimek Corbasi, an authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup, garnished with fresh mint.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a warm, flavorful bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup, perfect for a cozy meal.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its comforting warmth and rich flavor. This traditional recipe combines red lentils with aromatic vegetables and a special butter-mint topping, creating a wholesome and satisfying dish perfect for any meal. Enjoy a taste of authentic Turkish home cooking with this easy-to-follow recipe.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables
instructions.text: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and potato. Saute for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for another minute, stirring constantly. Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot and mix well with the vegetables.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in 6-7 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup until completely smooth. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth. Return the soup to the pot.

instructions.title: Season and Adjust Consistency
instructions.text: Return the pot to low heat. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Let it gently simmer for another 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Mint Butter Topping
instructions.text: While the soup simmers, melt butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (if using). Cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual serving bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the warm mint butter topping over each bowl. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, if desired.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, you can toast the dry lentils lightly in the pot for a minute or two before adding the liquid, which deepens their earthy taste.

The traditional Turkish red pepper flakes, 'Pul Biber' (also known as Maras pepper or Urfa pepper), offer a unique mild heat and rich flavor. If you cant find them, regular red pepper flakes can be used, but adjust quantity to taste.

Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day! It can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth if it has thickened too much.
article:
**Mercimek Corbasi: The Soul-Warming Embrace of Turkish Red Lentil Soup**

There are few dishes that evoke the feeling of warmth, comfort, and home quite like a bowl of traditional Turkish soup. Among the vast array of culinary delights from Turkey, one soup stands out as a universal favorite, a staple found on nearly every table, from humble kitchens to elegant restaurants: Mercimek Corbasi. This vibrant red lentil soup is more than just a meal; it's a hug in a bowl, a testament to the simplicity and richness of Turkish cuisine, offering a nourishing embrace with every spoonful.

Turkish soups and stews hold a special place in the country's gastronomic heart. They are often the starting point of any meal, designed to awaken the palate and prepare it for the feast to come. From hearty meat-based stews to delicate vegetable broths, the diversity is immense. But Mercimek Corbasi, with its striking color, creamy texture, and deeply satisfying flavor, has carved out a unique and beloved niche. It's a dish that crosses all social strata and is enjoyed year-round, though it's particularly cherished during colder months or when one craves something truly comforting and wholesome.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced mer-jee-MEK chor-BAH-suh, translates directly to "lentil soup." While other lentil soups exist in Turkish cuisine, when someone says "mercimek corbasi," they almost invariably mean the red lentil version. This iconic soup is characterized by its brilliant orange-red hue, its smooth, velvety texture, and a flavor profile that balances earthy lentils with aromatic vegetables, a hint of tomato, and a bright finish from lemon.

Its origins are deeply rooted in Anatolian tradition, where lentils have been a dietary cornerstone for centuries due to their affordability, nutritional value, and versatility. Red lentils, unlike green or brown varieties, break down quickly when cooked, making them ideal for creating a creamy, naturally thickened soup without the need for flour or cream. This makes Mercimek Corbasi not only delicious but also incredibly healthy, packed with protein, fiber, and essential nutrients. It's a testament to how simple, humble ingredients can be transformed into something truly extraordinary through time-honored cooking techniques. Whether served as a hearty breakfast, a light lunch, or the perfect appetizer to a larger meal, Mercimek Corbasi is a dish that nourishes both body and soul.

## Ingredients for Mercimek Corbasi

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its simplicity. You don't need a long list of exotic ingredients to create this culinary masterpiece. Most items are likely already in your pantry or easily found at any grocery store.

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Washed thoroughly, they form the creamy base of the soup.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation, providing depth and savory flavor.
*   **Carrot:** Adds a touch of sweetness, body, and enhances the red-orange color.
*   **Tomato Paste:** A crucial ingredient that contributes umami, richness, and helps deepen the soup's color.
*   **Olive Oil (or Butter):** For sautéing the aromatics and building the initial flavor.
*   **Water or Broth:** Chicken or vegetable broth can be used for added flavor, though water is traditional and still yields a delicious result.
*   **Mint (Dried):** A quintessential Turkish spice, dried mint adds a wonderfully fresh, aromatic note that elevates the soup.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** For a gentle warmth and a touch of color, often added to the final butter drizzle.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season the soup to perfection.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Essential for serving, a squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens all the flavors.

## Step-by-Step Mercimek Corbasi Recipe

Making this delightful soup is surprisingly straightforward. Here’s how you can bring the taste of Turkey into your kitchen:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup (about 200g) of red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any starch and impurities.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and 1 finely chopped small carrot, cooking for another 3-5 minutes until slightly tender.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste & Spices:** Stir in 1 tablespoon of tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint, 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (optional, adjust to taste), 1 teaspoon of salt, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper. Cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the aromatics and spices. Pour in 6 cups (about 1.5 liters) of water or broth. Bring the mixture to a boil.
5.  **Simmer:** Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have mostly broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a standard blender, being careful with hot liquids, and blend until smooth. Return the blended soup to the pot.
7.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed.
8.  **Prepare the Garnish (Optional but Recommended):** In a small pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint and 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (pul biber). Cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices.
9.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the spiced butter mixture over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges and crusty bread.

## Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving the perfect Mercimek Corbasi is an art that combines simple steps with a few key insights.

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This is crucial. Rinsing removes excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy or gummy. It also helps with digestion.
*   **Don't Skip the Tomato Paste Sauté:** Cooking the tomato paste for a minute or two before adding liquids caramelizes it slightly, deepening its flavor and adding a richer umami note to the soup.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** The signature texture of Mercimek Corbasi is its velvety smoothness. An immersion blender makes this easy, but a regular blender works just as well. Ensure there are no chunky bits left.
*   **The Spiced Butter Drizzle (Salça Sosu):** This final flourish is non-negotiable for an authentic experience. The sizzled butter with dried mint and red pepper flakes adds a layer of aroma, flavor, and visual appeal that truly elevates the soup. Be careful not to burn the spices in the butter.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice is Key:** Always serve with lemon wedges. A generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the table brightens the earthy flavors of the lentils and cuts through the richness, providing a refreshing counterpoint.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils absorb a lot of liquid. If your soup thickens too much after blending or sitting, simply whisk in a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your preferred consistency.

## Serving Suggestions for Mercimek Corbasi

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways.

*   **As an Appetizer:** This is its most traditional role in Turkey, serving as a warm and welcoming start to any meal.
*   **Light Lunch or Dinner:** Paired with a fresh green salad and some crusty Turkish bread (ekmek), it makes for a perfectly satisfying and healthy meal on its own.
*   **With Fresh Bread:** A must! A good crusty bread is ideal for soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup. Turkish pita bread or simit (sesame bread rings) are fantastic choices.
*   **Garnishes:** Beyond the spiced butter drizzle and lemon, consider a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or a few extra red pepper flakes for those who like more heat. A dollop of plain yogurt can also be a refreshing addition, especially if the soup is on the spicier side.
*   **Alongside Other Dishes:** It pairs wonderfully with grilled meats, kebabs, or a simple rice pilaf.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Mercimek Corbasi recipe is perfect as is, there's always room for personal touches and dietary adjustments.

*   **Vegan Option:** This soup is naturally vegan if you use vegetable broth or water instead of chicken broth, and olive oil instead of butter for sautéing. For the garnish, use a plant-based butter alternative or simply a drizzle of good quality olive oil infused with the spices.
*   **Add Vegetables:** For extra nutrients and texture, you can add other finely diced vegetables like potatoes, bell peppers, or even a small amount of celery along with the onion and carrot. Just ensure they are cooked until very tender before blending.
*   **Spice it Up:** If you love heat, increase the amount of red pepper flakes in both the soup and the butter garnish. You could also add a pinch of cayenne pepper.
*   **Different Herbs:** While dried mint is traditional and highly recommended, you could experiment with a pinch of dried oregano or thyme for a slightly different flavor profile. Fresh mint or parsley can be stirred in at the very end or used as a garnish.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A tiny pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth, complementing the earthy lentils beautifully.
*   **Sweet Potato:** For a sweeter and even creamier texture, replace the carrot with a small amount of finely diced sweet potato.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary embrace, a taste of Turkish hospitality, and a testament to the power of simple, wholesome ingredients. Its ability to comfort, nourish, and delight makes it a beloved dish across cultures. Whether you're seeking a healthy meal, a warming starter, or just a taste of something authentically delicious, this red lentil soup is sure to become a cherished addition to your recipe repertoire. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to be enchanted by the timeless charm of Mercimek Corbasi.
